methods.description: This method involves analyzing raw data acquired from Q-Exactive using MaxQuant for peptide-spectrum matching, generating an ion library with ProteinPilot, and quantifying proteins using PeakView. It is used to identify and quantify proteins in the samples.

steps.description: The raw data acquired on Q-Exactive were searched against two reference proteome files including the available G. hirsutum protein sequences in UniProtKB and the protein sequences obtained from G. robinsonii using MaxQuant for peptide-spectrum matching.
steps.searchable_summary: search raw LC-MS/MS data against reference proteomes using MaxQuant

steps.description: For ion library generation, data in the IDA mode were processed in ProteinPilot using the Paragon algorithm with default parameters against the reference proteome of G. robinsonii.
steps.searchable_summary: generate ion library using ProteinPilot with Paragon algorithm

steps.description: The obtained library was then imported into PeakView as a reference ion library and matched against individual SWATH data files.
steps.searchable_summary: import ion library into PeakView and match against SWATH data

steps.description: The peak areas for peptides were extracted by summing the area under curve values of the corresponding fragment ions using PeakView.
steps.searchable_summary: extract peak areas for peptides using PeakView

steps.description: The summed peak areas of the peptides were normalized against the total abundance values of respective samples and then used for protein quantification.
steps.searchable_summary: normalize peak areas and quantify proteins

steps.description: To assess differentially expressed proteins (DEPs), a comparison of protein abundances across respective sample groups was performed using two-sample Student's t tests.
steps.searchable_summary: perform t-tests to assess differentially expressed proteins

methods.description: This method involves identifying repetitive elements in the assembled genome using RepeatMasker and analyzing genome characteristics such as genome size estimation and GC content correlation.

steps.description: A total of 1 292 294 scaffolds with an overall length of 1 498 229 442 bp were input into RepeatMasker to identify repeats in the assembled genome using Repbase.
steps.searchable_summary: identify repeats in genome using RepeatMasker and Repbase

steps.description: K-mer analysis to estimate the genome size of Gossypium robinsonii by the formula: Genome size = K-mer number/peak depth.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate genome size using K-mer analysis

steps.description: The correlation analysis of guanine plus cytosine (GC) content and sequencing depth.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze correlation between GC content and sequencing depth
